title: Task 7: Integration test app
description: Create integration-test-graph.roc that seeds a SetProp, verifies full message lifecycle through the graph engine.

title: Task 6: End-to-end smoke test
description: Build graph-app.roc, link with Rust host, run the binary and verify timer handling works.

title: Task 3: Create Codec package for message encoding
description: Create packages/graph/codec/ with Codec.roc implementing encode/decode for NodeMessage and shard envelopes (QuineId + NodeMessage) as List U8.

title: Task 4: Codec roundtrip tests
description: Create CodecTest.roc with roundtrip tests for all message variants, edge cases, and error handling.

title: Task 5: Create graph-app.roc
description: Create app/graph-app.roc implementing the platform contract (init_shard!, handle_message!, on_timer!) by delegating to ShardState and interpreting effects via host functions.
close_reason: graph-app.roc created, spec-reviewed, compiles with 0 errors

title: Task 2: Add clear_effects to ShardState
description: Add clear_effects function to ShardState.roc that resets pending_effects to empty list. Needed by app layer after draining effects.

title: Task 1: Pass tag byte through shard_worker.rs
description: Change shard_worker.rs to pass full tagged message to Roc handle_message! instead of stripping the tag byte. Enables Roc to discriminate between shard messages (0x01) and persist results (0xFE).

title: Wire Phase 3b graph layer to Phase 3a platform
description: Connect the pure Roc graph layer (ShardState, dispatch, LRU, sleep/wake) to the Rust/tokio host platform. App.roc acts as the effect interpreter, Codec module handles List U8 encoding, shard_worker passes tag bytes through.

title: Phase 3a: Scaffold + ABI verification (Tasks 1-4)
description: Scaffold Cargo project, memory callbacks, platform declaration (main.roc/Host.roc/Effect.roc), and hello-world Roc<->Rust ABI verification. Highest-risk item — ABI is pre-1.0.
close_reason: Scaffold, memory callbacks, platform main.roc, and hello-world ABI verification all working. Review fixes applied (static linking, as_refcount_ptr).

title: Phase 3a: Effect executor + test apps (Tasks 11-14)
description: Effect drain skeleton, echo/timer/backpressure/persistence test apps.
close_reason: Effect executor skeleton, echo test (messages chain 0→1→2→3), backpressure test, persistence test all working.

title: Phase 3a: Runtime components (Tasks 8-10)
description: Shard worker recv-dispatch loop, tokio timer wheel for CheckLru, persistence I/O pool with persist_async!.
close_reason: Shard workers (std::thread recv loop), tokio timer wheel, persistence I/O pool all verified. Timer ticks fire correctly, persist round-trip works.

title: Phase 3a: Host functions (Tasks 5-7)
description: Implement current_time!, log!, and send_to_shard! host functions. Verifies roc_fx_* calling convention works (Roc calling into Rust).
close_reason: Host.roc, Effect.roc, current_time!, log!, send_to_shard! all verified working. ABI: hosted [fn!] syntax, raw U8 return at FFI boundary wrapped in Effect.roc.

title: Phase 3a: Cleanup and docs (Tasks 15-16)
description: CLI argument parsing, final wiring, commit ADR-016 update + ADR-017 + distribution analysis.
close_reason: CLI args, ADR-016/017, threading analysis, spec, and plan all committed.

title: Profile and optimize LRU eviction sort
description: Phase 3b ships with Option 2 (Dict + periodic re-sort every 10s). The sort is O(n log n) where n = awake nodes per shard (typically 5k-15k). This is acceptable initially but may become a bottleneck under high node churn. Future work: (1) Profile the sort under realistic load (50k+ awake nodes, 10s tick). (2) Evaluate alternative LRU structures: bucketed time windows or priority queue. (3) Consider Roc-specific algorithmic approaches given no doubly-linked list support. Sort key: (last_access asc, -cost_to_sleep desc).

title: Explore Level 2 multi-writer shard replication
description: Investigate multi-writer replication for shards — consensus or CRDTs for conflict resolution. Deferred from Phase 3a threading analysis. See refs/analysis/threading-distribution-matrix.md for context on Level 0/1/2 replication model.

value: Roc opaque type abilities (Hash, Eq) require explicit imports in the platform main.roc when Box.box is used on ShardState containing Dict with opaque keys (QuineId). Also requires explicit Hash implementation (not auto-derived) on the opaque type. Without this, the compiler can't resolve abilities across package boundaries.
